It's comming!

Yea, it's comming WAY sooner then you think. Thats right, ZELDA GAIDEN...RELEASE DATE: CHRISTMAS! YEA! THIS CHRISTMAS! NINTENDO REALLY PULLED IT OFF THIS TIME!

Full Report:

In what will probably come as a surprise to nobody, Nintendo's anticipated follow-up to The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time has been officially positioned for a holiday season 2000 release in the US. Nintendo expects the game, tentatively titled Zelda Gaiden, to be its best-selling title for Christmas.
"I can already predict that next year at this time I'll be standup up here announcing huge sales projections for the odds-on best-seller of Christmas 2000, the Nintendo 64 sequel to Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time," said Nintendo's Peter Main at a recent conference in New York.

While common sense told us that any Zelda sequel would make its way to every major international market, Main's words come as the first official indication that Gaiden is, in fact, US bound -- and targeted as the big holiday season title.

Zelda Gaiden, created by the same team that worked on Ocarina of Time (minus Shigeru Miyamoto), follows the adventures of Link after he awakens in a new world that feels and looks strangely familiar, but very different at the same time. The title is said to make use of a real-time night and day system that has a bearing on gameplay. Gaiden will also utilize the 4MB Expansion Pak for enhanced visual depth, more enemies on-screen and improved AI routines. The EAD created game is one of the first to fall under Nintendo's new philosophy of designing software under specific time restrictions by using and building upon existing game engines.

It is not known if Gaiden will still launch in Japan in Q1 2000 as previously announced, or if Nintendo will release the game simultaneously for the holidays.

More info soon.
